{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks whether the ability to perform transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) is influenced by the surgeon's previous experience. Reviewing the documents, Document [15] from the MEDCORP source states: 'Transurethral resection of the prostate is an art that requires substantial practice to master. Since urologists are performing fewer of these procedures now than in the past, it is more difficult for most to develop very high skill levels.' This directly indicates that TURP is a procedure that requires substantial practice and that skill development is dependent on experience. Additionally, Document [16] mentions that 'Unless very skilled and experienced, hold the resectoscope very still when cutting,' implying that experience is necessary to perform the procedure effectively. These statements support the idea that previous experience significantly influences a surgeon's ability to perform TURP. Therefore, the evidence strongly supports that the ability is influenced by experience.", "answer_choice": "A"}
